response:
Short answer: azr-compliance-v3 implements a dedicated, server‑side bulk remediation pipeline backed by Postgres and a timer worker (not by the existing sync_queue) so it can scale, recover, and be actively inspected from the UI.

How it works (key points):
- Dedicated run model: bulk jobs are persisted in Postgres tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items. A bulk "run" snapshots the full set of deployable subscriptions from the shared Event Grid coverage SQL when started.
- Dedicated worker loop: a 15‑second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation processes run_items. The worker is guarded by a Postgres advisory lock so only one worker instance owns a run at a time.
- No reuse of sync_queue: the design explicitly avoids the old sync_queue to prevent contention and to make the workflow resumable and observable.
- Concurrency and recovery: the system requeues stale "running" items back to pending on restart so interrupted runs can recover. There are worker‑specific env knobs and a recency index (migration added) to order items by recency for processing.
- Idempotence and run reuse: the bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run instead of creating duplicate work; it avoids recomputing deployable targets if an active run exists.
- Integration with Fleet/APIM: the backend can acquire a ClientSecretCredential token and call Fleet endpoints (via APIM) and poll returned statusUrl until completion for backend‑mediated flows where application permission is required. (For some single‑subscription UI flows the browser may call Fleet directly when the operator has an MSAL token.)
- UI/server coordination and hardening: UI state for the BulkRemediationPanel is sourced from the same paged queries as the run items to avoid mixed-run rendering; follow‑up fixes ensure correct ordering, better failure diagnostics, and robust behavior under load.

Together these choices let azr-compliance-v3 run large bulk Event Grid remediations reliably, recover from worker restarts, and scale without overloading legacy queueing paths.

Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest", "2026-04-09: azr-compliance-v3 Event Grid remediation was switched from a browser-side Fleet API call to a backend-mediated flow because Fleet requires application permission. The backend now acquires a ClientSecretCredential token for scope api://daf679b9-9134-42a1-9998-9cfac279747f/.default and calls https://apim.mscloud.rackspace.com/v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription, polling the returned statusUrl until completion. The frontend calls POST /api/event-grid/reme", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/cus", "2026-04-10: PR #76 review feedback on server-side Event Grid bulk remediation produced four concrete follow-up fixes: avoid computing deployable targets when an active bulk run already exists; order latest bulk remediation items by recency instead of ascending id; source the BulkRemediationPanel run/loading state from the same paged query as its items to avoid mixed-run rendering; and make the 'View Active Bulk Run' button a true read/refocus action instead of issuing another POST.", "2026-04-10: Created follow-up hardening for Event Grid bulk remediation after PR #77 review comments. The fix adds forward migration 58 for a recency index on event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items(run_id, updated_at DESC, id DESC), adds bulk-worker-specific Fleet polling env knobs, and requeues stale running items back to pending so a worker restart can recover interrupted runs.", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, redesigned Event Grid Details to distinguish deployment inventory from remediation coverage. GetEventGridDeployments now returns metadata.coverage with deployable_missing_event_grid_count, missing_monitoring_count, and arrays for deployable_subscriptions vs out_of_scope_subscriptions, derived from active customer subscriptions, monitoring entitlement, and Rackspace-managed Event Grid inventory. The frontend EventGridDetails page now shows explicit remediation ", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27, live prod validation after a large event burst in azr-compliance-v3 showed the register path and queue handoff were working, but sync_bulk_automation_execute was delayed behind serial queue processing until 21:07Z. After it ran, post-21:07 transport was clean: /api/bulk-operations/register returned 201 four times, /api/bulk-operations/callback returned 200 seven times, and there were zero response 500s in api_audit_all for that post-21:07 window. Bulk item state since 19:30Z then ", "2026-03-30: On 2026-03-30 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, corrected a bad bulk-operations UI fix. The user did not want parent policy invalid/failed diagnostics copied to child alert rows. Removed the child-alert inheritance changes from web-dashboard/src/pages/BulkOperations.jsx and instead added backend backfill in backend-py/GetBulkOperationItems/__init__.py so invalid policy rows with blank last_error derive a reason from subscription_compliance_comparison and azure_policy_assignments. Added integration t", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27 in azr-compliance-v3 develop, fixed two bulk automation issues. First, RegisterBulkOperation was inserting compatibility rows as status=sent with selected=false and never queueing sync_bulk_automation_execute; changed it to queue status, selected=true, and immediate enqueue with self-healing sync operation registration. Second, ExecuteBulkOperation now validates policy create items before publish: it blocks create when current local comparison no longer needs remediation, when the ", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding "]}
